---
description: "Deep-explain a DSA concept using a Biotrackr codebase example as the anchor"
agent: "DSA Mentor"
argument-hint: "concept name (e.g., 'sliding window', 'BFS')"
---

Explain the specified DSA concept from first principles, anchored to a concrete Biotrackr example.

## Explanation Structure

### Intuition

Describe the core idea in plain language before introducing any code or formal notation. Use a real-world analogy. Then map the analogy to the Biotrackr domain (health data, pagination, activity windows, conversation history, or similar).

### Biotrackr Example

Identify a real pattern in the Biotrackr codebase — or construct a plausible one — that either uses this concept correctly, could benefit from it, or illustrates why it matters. Show the relevant code snippet and explain how the concept applies.

### Implementation

Provide a clean C# implementation of the concept applied to the example above. The code must compile under .NET 10 and follow Biotrackr naming conventions (PascalCase methods, `_camelCase` fields).

```csharp
// Implementation here
```

### Complexity Analysis

| Dimension | Value | Notes |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| Time (average) | O(...) | Explanation |
| Time (worst) | O(...) | Explanation |
| Space | O(...) | Explanation |

### When NOT to Use This Concept

List at least two conditions under which this concept is the wrong choice. Include the preferred alternative for each condition and why it is better in that context.

### Quick-Check Questions

Provide three short questions the learner can answer to verify understanding. Do not include answers — leave them for the learner to work through.
